Remediation plan for finding sg-finding-docker-env-002
  Secret kind: OpenAI API Key
  Severity:    high
  Preview:     sk-t...3456
  Fingerprint: sha256:bbccddeeff22

Steps:

  1. Remove the secret from Dockerfile ENV
     ENV instructions bake secrets into the image layer. Any user with image access can retrieve them via `docker history` or `docker inspect`.
     $ Remove the ENV line containing the secret from the Dockerfile.

  2. Use build-time secrets instead
     Pass secrets at build time using `docker build --secret`. The secret is available in the build context but not persisted in the image.
     $ docker build --secret id=mysecret,src=./mysecret.txt -t myimage .

  3. Use a local env file with restricted permissions
     Store secrets in a .env file (chmod 600) and load them in the container at runtime. Ensure the .env file is in .dockerignore.
     $ echo SECRET=value > .env && chmod 600 .env

  ⚠ Do not delete containers or images based on this plan alone — always coordinate with the team first.

  ⚠ After rotating credentials, rebuild and redeploy any affected images or containers.

  ⚠ Review all CI/CD pipelines that might have access to the exposed credential.
